reason: The UI has moved from generic swiping to contextual profile decisions, but the persisted collection remains swipes until a dual-read, dual-write, and backfill migration is worth the operational risk.

phases.description: Keep the existing swipes storage path, expose generated logical path constants, and fail data-contract checks if production code adds new raw path literals.
Repositories and triggers read both swipes and profileDecisions, with swipes remaining the primary write path.
Client and backend writes mirror new decisions into both storage paths with idempotent writes.

Dry-run-first tooling copies historical swipes into profileDecisions and compares source/target counts before apply. Dev, staging, and prod had zero current and future decisions on 2026-05-15, so no writes were needed. Rerun before any trigger cutover while legacy clients can still write swipes.
profileDecisions becomes the primary read/write path while swipes remains a fallback read path.
New writes to swipes stop after parity checks prove profileDecisions has complete data.
Remove swipes fallback reads and delete old data only after explicit production approval.

guards: tool/check_schema_path_literals.mjs blocks new production raw swipes collection and trigger literals.
./tool/check_data_contract.sh runs the path literal scanner before seed and rules checks.

tool/validate_profile_decision_migration.mjs compares current and future storage paths, validates both sides against the generated decision schema, and can fail with --require-parity before a primary cutover.
No live storage rename is allowed until a count-comparison backfill tool exists.
